name: Oban 14 Year
notes: honey, orchard fruit, almond fruitcake, orange, brine, smoke, menthol, dark chocolate, apple
best: Balanced Scotch, medium-full cigar, coastal character.
why: Recommend when the member wants one Scotch that does fruit and light smoke — orange, honey, sea salt.
catalogLine: Oban 14 Year · West Highland, Scotland · $85 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: orange peel, honey
blurb: Oban 14 — orange and honey with a touch of West Highland smoke and sea. One glass that covers fruit and peat without extremes.

name: Old Forester 1920 Prohibition Style
mash: Corn, rye, malted barley (Whiskey Row series)
notes: cherry preserves, caramel, dark chocolate, maple syrup, spiced nuts, malt, nougat, rye spice, cedar, toasted coconut, charred marshmallow, oak spice
best: Experienced bourbon drinker, maduro pairing, user wants proof and dark fruit.
avoid: Beginner, empty stomach, or user wants mild Connecticut-only pairing.
why: Recommend when the member wants a stronger bourbon for maduro — dark cherry, chocolate, and controlled heat.
catalogLine: Old Forester 1920 Prohibition Style · Kentucky · $65 · 115 proof (57.5% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: dark cherry, chocolate
blurb: Old Forester 1920 — cherry preserves and caramel over dark chocolate, maple, and toasted coconut at 115 proof. Distillery-official maduro partner.

name: Patrón Silver
best: Blanco request, margarita, clean pairing with mild cigar.
why: Recommend when the member wants crisp agave — citrus, pepper, and mineral.
catalogLine: Patrón Silver · Jalisco, Mexico · $45 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: fresh agave, citrus
blurb: Patrón Silver — clean agave and citrus in a blanco everyone recognizes. The tequila pour for margaritas and mild cigars alike.

name: Penelope Bourbon
notes: corn sweetness, caramel, light oak, fruit, berry, citrus, vanilla, buttercream, corn bread
research: Official site describes four-grain bourbon profile; lounge SKU Penelope Bourbon.
best: Younger member, trendy bourbon ask, medium cigar.
avoid: Purist who rejects sourced whiskey only.
why: Recommend when the member asks for Penelope or wants modern approachable bourbon.
catalogLine: Penelope Bourbon · Kentucky / Indiana · $42 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: caramel, vanilla
blurb: Penelope Bourbon — caramel and orchard fruit in a modern pour lounges actually stock. Approachable premium without the allocated chase.

name: Pikesville Straight Rye
notes: cocoa, oaky smoke, honeyed rye, cloves, vanilla, baking spices
best: Serious rye drinker, full-bodied cigar, bonded-style strength.
why: Recommend when 110-proof rye should match a full-bodied cigar — spice, dark fruit, and pepper.
catalogLine: Pikesville Straight Rye · Kentucky · $45 · 110 proof (55.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: rye spice, dark fruit
blurb: Pikesville Rye — 110-proof Kentucky rye with dark fruit and pepper. The rye pour when Sazerac is not enough.

name: Plantation O.F.T.D. Overproof Rum
mash: Blend of pot & column Caribbean rums
notes: molasses, dark chocolate, banana, spice, coffee, oak
research: Planteray OFTD official blend description; overproof navy-style.
best: Member wants maximum rum intensity, bold cigar, tiki-adjacent strength.
why: Recommend when the cigar is full-bodied and the rum must keep up — molasses, chocolate, 138 proof.
catalogLine: Plantation O.F.T.D. Overproof Rum · Caribbean blend · $35 · 138 proof (69.0% ABV) · Notes: molasses, dark chocolate
blurb: Plantation OFTD — molasses and chocolate at 138 proof. The rum that refuses to disappear beside a ligero maduro.

name: Redbreast 12 Year
notes: spicy fruit, sherry, toasted wood, silky, harmonious
best: Irish curious, pot still request, sherry-friendly cigar.
avoid: User wants Scotch or bourbon only.
why: Recommend when the member has not tried Irish — sherry, spice, and pot still beside a maduro.
catalogLine: Redbreast 12 Year · Ireland · $65 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: sherry, baked apple
blurb: Redbreast 12 — sherry and baking spice from Irish pot still. Maybe the best whiskey category you were missing for cigar pairing.

name: Rémy Martin XO
notes: white flowers, candied fruit, ripe plum, candied orange, nutmeg, hazelnut, roasted cocoa, honeyed gingerbread
best: Member wants elegant cognac for cigar, fruit-forward luxury, Rémy name request.
avoid: User wants maximum smoke or tequila only.
why: Recommend when silky cognac beside cigar matters — fig, plum, honey, and fine champagne finesse.
catalogLine: Rémy Martin XO · Cognac, France · $190 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: fig, jasmine
blurb: Rémy Martin XO — fig, plum, and jasmine over silky oak. The cognac that whispers luxury beside a maduro instead of shouting it.

name: Rittenhouse Straight Rye Bottled-in-Bond
notes: dried fruits, toffee, sweet peppers, cocoa, citrus, cinnamon, nutmeg, vanilla, maple spice
best: Rye cocktail order, spice-forward cigar, budget rye with backbone.
why: Recommend for bonded rye beside a peppery cigar — spice, mint, and chocolate at 100 proof.
catalogLine: Rittenhouse Straight Rye Bottled-in-Bond · Kentucky · $28 · 100 proof (50.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: rye spice, dark chocolate
blurb: Rittenhouse BIB — bonded rye with pepper, mint, and dark chocolate. The bartender's rye that still holds up neat beside a spicy cigar.

name: Russell's Reserve 10 Year
notes: orange peel, citrus, butterscotch, maple, oak, vanilla, caramel, spice, toffee
best: Turkey fan, age-stated value, medium-full cigar.
avoid: User wants rye or Scotch only.
why: Recommend when the member trusts Wild Turkey DNA but wants refined 10-year oak.
catalogLine: Russell's Reserve 10 Year · Kentucky · $45 · 90 proof (45.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: caramel, vanilla
blurb: Russell's Reserve 10 — caramel and toffee from a decade in oak. Wild Turkey pedigree in a quieter, cigar-friendly register.

name: Sazerac Rye
notes: clove, vanilla, anise, pepper, candied spice, citrus, licorice
best: Rye curious beginner, Old Fashioned/Sazerac order, budget-friendly rye.
avoid: User wants 100+ proof or heavy peat.
why: Recommend as the approachable rye entry — spice and honey without WhistlePig intensity.
catalogLine: Sazerac Rye · Kentucky · $32 · 90 proof (45% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: rye spice, honey
blurb: Sazerac Rye — clove, vanilla, and anise over pepper, citrus, and licorice. Distillery-official rye for the namesake cocktail or a neat pour.

name: Smirnoff No. 21 Vodka
notes: neutral, light citrus, grain, mineral, smooth, faint sweetness
research: Top value-tier vodka contributor in 2025 US sales (Royal Batch).
best: Budget vodka cocktails, party pours, flavored-margarita base.
avoid: User wants premium wheat vodka character only.
why: Recommend for value vodka cocktails — neutral base that will not fight a Connecticut cigar.
catalogLine: Smirnoff No. 21 Vodka · USA (multi-origin) · $16 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: neutral, light citrus
blurb: Smirnoff No. 21 — the value vodka America still buys in volume. Neutral, mixable, and honest about what it is.

name: Talisker 10 Year
notes: peat smoke, sea salt, citrus, dried fruit, barley malt, pepper, honey
best: Peppery cigar, coastal smoke, member wants peat with spice.
why: Recommend when pepper in the cigar should meet pepper and maritime smoke in the glass.
catalogLine: Talisker 10 Year · Isle of Skye, Scotland · $60 · 91.6 proof (45.8% ABV) · Notes: pepper, smoke
blurb: Talisker 10 — black pepper and sea salt over maritime smoke. The Scotch that loves a spicy Nicaraguan as much as you do.

name: Tanqueray No. Ten
best: Martini order, citrus-forward gin, summer lounge.
why: Recommend when a martini matters — grapefruit, juniper, and citrus peel.
catalogLine: Tanqueray No. Ten · England · $38 · 94.6 proof (47.3% ABV) · Notes: grapefruit, orange peel
blurb: Tanqueray No. Ten — grapefruit and juniper built for martinis. The gin pour when someone wants citrus beside a light cigar.
